title: megalastrum littorale
intro: Megalastrum littorale, also known as the large-leafed fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Ophioglossaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Megalastrum littorale include its potential to treat various health conditions such as skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves can be applied topically to the skin for therapeutic purposes.
